title: Missing Hdr Ext Len calculation in SRH write
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/routing.c:83-113

Even if the partial SRH format (starting at routing-type byte) is intentional, the code doesn't help the caller compute Hdr Ext Len which is required per RFC 6554.

RFC 6554 defines Hdr Ext Len as: (n * 2) where n = number of 128-bit addresses, if CmprI=CmprE=0 (uncompressed).

The caller must compute this independently, which is error-prone. Consider adding a helper function:
```c
static inline uint8_t lichen_rpl_srh_hdr_ext_len(uint8_t num_addresses) {
    return num_addresses * 2;  // uncompressed
}
```

Impact: Callers may incorrectly compute Hdr Ext Len leading to malformed packets.
